overall_reasoning: The implementation matches the requested error semantics precisely: all operational failure categories enumerated in the task flow into Result.failure through a single try/catch boundary per module, while the one caller-argument validation case (incomplete GOFS drop-off coordinate pairs) still throws IllegalArgumentException synchronously before feed lookup, URL building, or any network request, and is tested with a zero-request assertion. The patch is complete across public API and tests, ABI snapshots are regenerated and verified, and executed JVM/native test suites pass. The aggregate build failure is attributable to an external download DNS error, not to the code. Remaining issues are stylistic (slightly duplicated helper overloads, stale per-method KDoc, no cancellation test) and do not rise to blockers.

rating_rationales.code_clarity: The shared getFeedResponse boundary is small and duplicated identically across modules, which fits the existing per-module structure. Minor clarity costs: the lambda-deferred URL construction is slightly indirect, GOFS keeps two near-duplicate overloads, and per-method KDoc @return text still describes unwrapped responses.
rating_rationales.repository_fit: Follows the established module layout, MockEngine test style, and ABI snapshot workflow; .api and .klib.api files were regenerated with the project's own Gradle tasks and pass checkKotlinAbi.
rating_rationales.scope_discipline: Changes are confined to the four client classes, their tests, doc snippets, and regenerated ABI/API snapshot files. No unrelated modules, dependencies, or behavior were touched.
rating_rationales.security_and_safety: Operational catch is limited to Exception, cancellation is rethrown, argument validation cannot be swallowed by the Result boundary, and no credentials, unsafe networking changes, or injection-prone behavior were introduced.
rating_rationales.test_quality: Each module gains focused tests for transport, HTTP error status, decode failure, and missing feed, with concrete exception-type assertions for feed lookup and request-count proof that GOFS invalid coordinates throw before the engine runs. Existing producer tests were updated to unwrap success. Minor gaps: no cancellation-rethrow test and the transport tests assert only isFailure rather than the propagated exception.

strengths: Consistent Result-returning transformation applied to every public client method across GBFS v1, v2, v3, and GOFS v1, including both direct-URL manifest calls and context-based feed calls
Feed-URL lookup is deferred into the try/catch lambda so missing-feed NoSuchElementException correctly becomes Result.failure as required
CancellationException is explicitly rethrown and only Exception (not Throwable) is caught, preserving coroutine cancellation and fatal-error semantics
GOFS coordinate-pair require() runs before the getFeedResponse boundary, so invalid arguments throw synchronously with no engine interaction, verified by request-count assertions
New ClientFailureTest suites in all four modules cover transport, HTTP status, decoding, and feed-lookup failures; GOFS additionally covers both incomplete-coordinate entry points
JVM and linuxX64 test suites plus ABI checks were run and passed; API snapshots (.api and .klib.api) were regenerated and validated
summary: The patch converts all public Ktor client methods in GBFS v1-v3 and GOFS v1 to return Result, mapping transport/HTTP/decoding/feed-lookup exceptions to Result.failure while keeping GOFS coordinate-pair validation as an immediate throw before any request. API dumps, producer tests, doc snippets, and new failure-semantics tests are included and the work was committed. Verification shows JVM tests, Linux native tests, all-target compilation, and ABI checks passing; the only failed check was the aggregate build blocked by an external DNS failure downloading V8, which is environmental and not caused by this change.
